entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The legume crop being investigated for male sterility.

entities.name: MICROSPOROGENESIS
entities.description: The biological process of pollen formation.

entities.name: GENIC-CYTOPLASMIC MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A form of male sterility resulting from the interaction of nuclear and cytoplasmic genes.

entities.name: SPOROGENOUS TISSUE
entities.description: The tissue that gives rise to pollen mother cells.

entities.name: PMCS
entities.description: Pollen Mother Cells that undergo meiosis.

entities.name: TAPETUM
entities.description: The innermost layer of the anther wall that provides nutrients to developing pollen.

entities.name: TETRADS
entities.description: Groups of four haploid cells resulting from meiosis.

entities.name: MEIOTIC FAILURE
entities.description: The breakdown of the meiotic process leading to non-viable pollen.

entities.name: POLLEN GRAIN
entities.description: The male gametophyte produced during microsporogenesis.

entities.name: VACUOLATION
entities.description: The formation of vacuoles within the pollen grain during meiotic failure.

entities.name: GENETIC MALE STERILITY
entities.description: Male sterility caused solely by nuclear genetic factors.

relationships.description: The study investigates the process of microsporogenesis in pigeonpea.
The crop exhibits genic-cytoplasmic male sterility.
The trait affects the progression of microsporogenesis.
Microsporogenesis begins with the development of sporogenous tissue.
Sporogenous tissue progresses into pollen mother cells.
Pollen mother cells develop into tetrads.
The disintegration of the tapetum is associated with the progression of PMC development.
Meiotic failure is a characteristic of the genic-cytoplasmic male sterile line.
Meiotic failure leads to the separation of cytoplasm from the pollen grain wall.
Vacuolation occurs within the pollen grain following meiotic failure.
The timing of meiotic failure in genic-cytoplasmic male sterility is compared to genetic male sterility.
